DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ALI ARI ALAEI, Esq. of Ari Law, PC (Tel: 415-830-9968) for plaintiff;
Atty. MICHAEL J. BARTLETT, Esq. of Sinnott, Puebla, Capagne & Curet, APLC (Tel: 213-996-4200) for defendant Steadfast Insurance Company;
Atty. KENNETH D. WATNICK, Esq. of Anderson, McPharlin & Conners LLP (Tel: 213-688-0080) for defendants Arthur J. Gallagher & Co., and Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. Insurance Brokers of California, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT STEADFAST INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Steadfast Insurance Co.'s five demurrers to the first amended complaint (FAC) are sustained in part and overruled in part.

First, the FAC adequately pleads assignment from West Builders, Inc. to Museum Plaza LLC on its first two pages. Overruled.

Second/Third, other parties may be found culpable with Steadfast. Thus, equitable indemnity and contribution claims are appropriate at the pleading stage. Further, as shown by CCP 877, culpability may be spread among a mix of tortfeasors and contract obligors. Overruled.

Fourth, an insurer and insured have a "special relationship," but the insurer is not a fiduciary. (Love v. Fire Ins. Exchange (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1136, 1148.) Thus, the demurrer to the FAC's "breach of fiduciary duty" count is sustained without leave to amend.

Fifth, the FAC alleges fraud, but fails to plead it with the required specificity. (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Sustained with leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Connie Parchman, CSR No. 6137, Email: parchaman1@gmail.com, Tel: 415-981-3498 (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ALI ARI ALAEI, Esq. of Ari Law, PC (Tel: 415-830-9968) for plaintiff;
Atty. MICHAEL J. BARTLETT, Esq. of Sinnott, Puebla, Capagne & Curet, APLC (Tel: 213-996-4200) for defendant Steadfast Insurance Company;
Atty. KENNETH D. WATNICK, Esq. of Anderson, McPharlin & Conners LLP (Tel: 213-688-0080) for defendants Arthur J. Gallagher & Co., and Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. Insurance Brokers of California, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS ARTHUR J. GALLAGHER & CO., ARTHUR J. GALLAGHER & CO. INSURANCE BROKERS OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. and Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. Insurance Brokers of California, Inc.'s (Brokers) demurrer on statute of limitations grounds to the first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained with prejudice. Hence, the demurrer's other grounds need not be reached.

The FAC pleads that Brokers are liable because "Subguard" insurance did not properly cover claims regarding a construction project by West Builders. The statute of limitations is two years. (CCP 339(1); Hydro-Mill Co., Inc. v. Hayward, Tilton and Rolapp Ins. Assoc., Inc. (2004) 115 Cal.App.4th 1145, 1153.) Yet the FAC pleads that West incurred "substantial damages" as early as 2008-09 and certainly by 2013 ("damages as a proximate result of Gallagher's negligence"). (Id. at 3:12-15, 4:16-18, 8:12-17; see also RJN Ex.1.) Plaintiff argues that "equitable exceptions to the statutes of limitations apply," but fails to articulate how that is supposedly so. (Opp. 11:4-5, 13:2-24.) The requests for judicial notice are granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with cRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Connie Parchman, CSR No. 6137, Email: parchaman1@

Defendant Museum Plaza, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Integrated Design Engineers, Llc Into Arbitration Pursuant To Written Stipulation And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Integrated Design Engineers, Llc And Its Counsel Of Record Pursuant To Ccp 128.5
Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NICK MERRELL, ESQ. of Varela, Lee, Metz & Guarino, LLP, Tel No. 415-623-7000 appearing for Plaintiff West Builders, Inc.;
Atty. J. ANDREW LAWSON, ESQ. of Severson & Werson APC, Tel No. 415-398-3344 appearing for Defendant Integrated Design Engineers;
Atty. LUCAS OLONA, ESQ. of Berding & Weil, Tel No. 925-838-2090 appearing for Defendant Museum Plaza, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC.'S Motion To Compel Integrated Design Engineers, LLC Into Arbitration Pursuant To Written Stipulation And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Integrated Design Engineers, LLC And Its Counsel Of Record Pursuant To CCP 128.5, Defendant Museum Plaza, LLC's Motion to Compel Arbitration is granted. Defendant Integrated Design Engineers signed agreement to arbitrate the instant controversy. (Decl. of Lucas J. Olona, Ex. A.) Five out of the six named parties to the arbitration agreement executed the agreement. The agreement, therefore, "reflects a clear intent to submit the case to arbitration . . . ." (Porreco v. Red Top RV Center (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 113, 120.) Defendant Integrated Design Engineers does not meet its burden to demonstrate that the arbitration agreement excludes the present dispute from arbitration. It does not sufficiently assert a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact. (Code Civ. Pro. Sec. 1281.2.) Defendant Museum Plaza LLC's Motion for Monetary Sanctions is denied. This Court has not previously ordered arbitration. In 2010, it merely stayed litigation pending arbitration. D Integrated Design Engineers, therefore, did not necessarily act in bad faith by opposing arbitration. There is insufficient evidence presented of Plaintiff's alleged unsuccessful attempts to meet and confer with Defendant Integrated Design Engineers. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
